findings.title: Config snapshot retirement leaks memory across dynamic updates
findings.impact: Every sampler config change exchanges in a new snapshot and appends the old one to oldSnapshots_, but production code only frees that vector during Shutdown. A long-running worker/client that receives repeated dynamic sampler updates or worker register/heartbeat config changes will retain one snapshot per change for the lifetime of the process, eventually creating unbounded memory growth and possible OOM.
findings.fix: Replace the raw snapshot retirement list with a safe reclamation scheme: for example publish snapshots through shared ownership/RCU epoch protection, or periodically reclaim retired snapshots after readers have quiesced, instead of keeping all old snapshots until shutdown.
